skill.name: azure-ai-formrecognizer-java
skill.description: Build Java applications with Azure Document Intelligence, formerly Form Recognizer. Use it to extract text, tables, key-value pairs, receipt data, invoice fields, and custom model results from documents.
skill.summary: Use Azure Document Intelligence Java SDK patterns for document analysis, custom models, and classification.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many backtick, URL, and weak-crypto patterns, but review found these are Markdown formatting, Java SDK examples, placeholder URLs, and ordinary description strings. No prompt injection, malicious intent, credential exfiltration, or executable installer behavior was found in SKILL.md. The skill still documents network-capable Azure SDK usage, so it is low risk rather than fully risk-free.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Matches Are Markdown Formatting
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick detections occur on Markdown code fences and inline code examples in SKILL.md. They do not execute commands and do not create a command injection path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Every cited location is inside Markdown code fencing or inline formatting. The file is documentation only and contains no Ruby runtime or shell execution wrapper.

low_findings.title: Placeholder Network URLs In SDK Examples
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are placeholders for an example invoice and an Azure Cognitive Services endpoint. Network access is expected for this Azure SDK skill, but the examples do not send secrets to an unknown endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible placeholders used to demonstrate Azure Document Intelligence calls. No evidence found of covert exfiltration, encoded payloads, or hidden remote command behavior.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto Alerts Are Keyword False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts do not identify cryptographic code. The cited lines describe Form Recognizer, a custom invoice model, and a composed model description.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain natural-language descriptions and model metadata strings. No hash functions, ciphers, or cryptographic API calls are present at these locations.

content.user_title: Build Azure Document Analysis in Java
content.value_statement: Document extraction projects need reliable SDK patterns for PDFs, receipts, invoices, and custom forms. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused Java guidance for Azure Document Intelligence workflows.
actual_capabilities: Shows Maven dependency setup for the Azure AI Form Recognizer Java package.
Demonstrates layout extraction, receipt analysis, invoice analysis, and general document key-value extraction.
Explains custom model build, analysis, composition, listing, deletion, and resource limit checks.
Covers document classifier build and classification calls with Java SDK patterns.
Includes basic HttpResponseException handling and environment variable names.
limitations: It is a reference skill, not a runnable application or test suite.
It uses placeholder endpoints, keys, model IDs, URLs, and document paths.
It does not validate current Azure SDK version compatibility.
It does not cover compliance review for documents containing sensitive personal data.

use_cases.title: Prototype Invoice Extraction
use_cases.description: Create Java code that sends invoice documents to Azure Document Intelligence and reads structured fields.

use_cases.title: Build Custom Document Models
use_cases.description: Plan Java administration calls for training, composing, listing, and deleting custom document models.

use_cases.title: Classify Document Sets
use_cases.description: Use classifier examples to route receipts, invoices, and other forms before deeper analysis.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Client
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a minimal Java Azure Document Intelligence client. Show the Maven dependency, credential setup, endpoint configuration, and one layout analysis call.

prompt_templates.title: Extract Receipt Fields
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to write Java code for analyzing a receipt URL. Include merchant name, transaction date, line items, prices, confidence values, and basic error handling.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Custom Model Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to outline a Java workflow for building a custom invoice model from Azure Blob training data, analyzing a document, and listing model details.

prompt_templates.title: Implement Document Routing
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a Java document classification pipeline. Include classifier creation, classification from URL, model selection, and operational checks for errors and resource limits.

output_examples.input: Analyze an invoice PDF with Azure Document Intelligence in Java.
output_examples.output: A Java implementation plan that creates a DocumentAnalysisClient, submits the invoice to a prebuilt model, reads extracted fields, reports confidence values, and handles service errors.

output_examples.input: Create a custom document model workflow.
output_examples.output: A step-by-step Java workflow covering Blob training data, model build options, model identifiers, analysis with the trained model, and model administration checks.

output_examples.input: Route mixed document types before extraction.
output_examples.output: A classifier-based design that separates invoices and receipts, selects the right analysis model, and records document type confidence for downstream processing.

best_practices: Store Azure endpoints and keys in environment variables or managed identity configuration.
Check confidence values before using extracted fields in automated decisions.
Use representative training documents and clear prefixes when building custom models.
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode production API keys in Java source files.
Do not send regulated documents to Azure without reviewing privacy and compliance requirements.
Do not delete or overwrite custom models without checking resource usage and deployment dependencies.

faq.question: Does this skill run Azure Document Intelligence by itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides Java SDK guidance and examples that a developer can adapt in their own project.

faq.question: Which Azure service does it target?
faq.answer: It targets Azure Document Intelligence, formerly known as Azure Form Recognizer.

faq.question: Can it help with invoices and receipts?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes examples for prebuilt receipt, invoice, document, layout, business card, ID, and W2 models.

faq.question: Does it support custom models?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers building, analyzing, composing, listing, inspecting, and deleting custom document models.

faq.question: Does it include authentication guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows AzureKeyCredential, DefaultAzureCredential, and environment variable names for endpoint and key configuration.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with low risk. Static findings were false positives, and the remaining concern is normal network use for Azure SDK workflows.
